title: castilleja parvula var. revealii
intro: Castilleja parvula var. revealii, also known as a rare and unique variety of the Western Indian Paintbrush, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant is known for its vibrant red and yellow flowers, which resemble a paintbrush, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Castilleja parvula var. revealii is said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate cold and flu symptoms, as well as reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into a tea or tincture, and may be used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, sore throats, and digestive problems.
